jurisdiction_name: United States - Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA)
description: COPPA protects the privacy of children under 13 in the United States

young_child.description: Verifiable parental consent required for children under 13

child_boundary.description: Child's own consent sufficient at age 13

parental_notice.description: Must provide clear notice to parents about information collection

parental_consent.description: Must obtain verifiable parental consent before collecting data from children under 13

parental_access.description: Parents must be able to review and request deletion of child's data

no_conditioning.description: Cannot condition participation in educational activity on collection of more data than needed

no_marketing.description: Cannot use child's data for marketing or third-party marketing

location_data.description: Location tracking generally not allowed for children under 13 without parental consent

photos_videos_audio.description: Cannot collect identifiable photos, videos, or audio from children under 13

persistent_identifiers.description: Cannot use cookies or persistent identifiers to track children under 13

safe_harbor.description: FTC safe harbor programs available for compliance

warnings: COPPA applies to children under 13 - verify all students' ages
Verifiable parental consent is the gold standard - email alone may not suffice
Cannot use persistent identifiers or cookies to track children under 13
Cannot sell or market children's data
Must delete data upon parental request
FTC has enforcement power and significant penalty potential ($43,280+ per violation)
requirements: Obtain verifiable parental consent before collecting data from children under 13
Provide parents with notice of data practices
Allow parents to access, review, and request deletion of child's data
Do not collect more information than necessary
Do not condition participation on data collection
Do not market or share data for marketing purposes
Have privacy policy clearly stating data practices
